label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 11

extraction.one_line_summary: HERRADA Jesús won from 26-rider breakaway after GANNA set infernal pace on finale climb, caught CAICEDO's flamme rouge attack.
tactical_patterns: GANNA paced breakaway climb so destructively for THOMAS that legitimate contenders like GODON were shed, revealing team time-trial power transfer.
CAICEDO attacked at flamme rouge but three chasers (THOMAS, HERRADA, GRÉGOIRE) coordinated to close rather than counter-attacking each other.
UIJTDEBROEKS went early from GC group targeting top-10 position defense, forcing CARTHY to chase for position protection rather than recovery.
GC favorites (EVENEPOEL, KUSS, ROGLIČ) neutralized each other's attacks on summit finish, suggesting mutual fear prevents selection on 14-16% ramps.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: paced breakaway climb day after winning time trial, demonstrating exceptional recovery capacity for sustained domestique work on steep gradients
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: stayed with GANNA's destructive pace then marked winning move, positioning for top-10 overall rather than stage hunting
rider_signals.CAICEDO Jonathan Klever: neutral: timed flamme rouge attack well but lacked repeatability when caught, suggests single-punch climbing profile
rider_signals.UIJTDEBROEKS Cian: positive: proactively attacked from GC group for time gaps when not obligated, showing tactical maturity for top-10 defense
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: forced to chase UIJTDEBROEKS for position defense rather than recovering, revealing vulnerable GC ranking
